mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of existing homes and new housing starts indicate a collapsing residential investment cycle that drags down aggregate demand and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the signal predictive power vanishes in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly expansions in real GDP reflect broad economic growth that reinforces investor confidence and generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observations, or the autocorrelation of returns negates the GDP signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI inflation above 3.5% annually forces the Federal Reserve to prioritize price stability over growth, raising discount rates and producing negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% annualized threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy in recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real existing home sales during periods of negative GDP growth signals a severe demand shock that accelerates risk aversion and triggers negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during negative GDP growth periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance in recessionary samples.
